items.title: Default to the cheaper GPT-5.6 tiers and escalate only when the task earns it
items.why: Tiered reasoning lets teams buy successful outcomes instead of paying frontier rates for every coding request.
how_to_steps: Define task classes first—routine edits, medium-depth coding, and hardest multi-tool runs—before you pick a default model.
Start routine coding loops on Luna or Terra and reserve Sol with higher reasoning effort for work that genuinely needs stronger judgment.
Use multi-agent or ultra-style parallelism only on demanding tasks where faster time-to-result is worth the extra token spend.
Track cost per successful task, retries, and human correction rate so routing follows outcomes instead of habit or raw token price.
items.evidence: OpenAI reports GPT-5.6 Sol hitting 80 on the Artificial Analysis Coding Agent Index, 2.8 points above Claude Fable 5 while using 54% fewer output tokens, taking 57% less time, and costing about one-third less; Terra and Luna preserve much of that coding quality at far lower price points. Lovable says GPT-5.6 cut steps by about 25%, tool calls by 35-48%, and stuck runs by 15%.
items.try_today: Route one coding workflow to Terra by default, escalate only when tests fail or the task stalls, and compare cost per accepted change after a day.
